title: Marbella Affordable Yacht Charter: Costs, Boats & Tips for 2026
html:
Whether you dream of cruising the Costa del Sol aboard a sleek motor yacht or simply skimming the waves on a jet ski, Marbella offers accessible boating for almost any budget. The prime departure point is Puerto Banús, with bareboat and skippered charter prices for a half-day typically ranging from 350 to 1,200 EUR. BoatHire24 connects you with local owners to find the perfect rental.

How much does it cost to charter a boat in Marbella?

A half-day shared tour costs from 85 EUR per person, while a private small motorboat half-day starts at around 400 EUR. Catamaran full-day rentals range from 1,500 to 2,800 EUR, and a crewed luxury yacht (up to 12 guests) can cost 3,000, 5,000 EUR per day. Prices vary by season and vessel size, so compare verified listings to find the best deal. For an affordable option, browse skippered charters in Marbella, where you can book a private trip with a captain included.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most charters depart from Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina built around a central square. Other excellent departure points include the Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Marbella), the smaller Cabopino Marina near the Artola dunes, and La Bajadilla fishing port. A popular day cruise takes you east to the secluded beaches of Calahonda and then west toward the upscale marina at Sotogrande. For a shorter trip, head south-west to the beaches of San Pedro de Alcántara.

Which boat to rent

  1. Jet ski (Sea-Doo GTX), best for adrenaline seekers; 1, 3 guests; from 140 EUR for 30 minutes. For details, visit the Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski charter page.
  2. Small motorboat (approx. 23, 30 ft), ideal for a couple or small family; up to 6 guests; from 400 EUR half-day.
  3. Catamaran (Lagoon or similar), stable and spacious for parties and sunbathing; up to 12 guests; from 1,500 EUR full-day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (Sunseeker, Azimut, or Fairline), luxury cruising with skipper and host; up to 12 guests; from 3,000 EUR full-day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

For bareboat rentals (no skipper) you need a valid ICC or equivalent licence recognised in Spain. Spain typically requires the International Certificate of Competence (ICC) issued by your home country's maritime authority. However, many charters include a skipper who holds the required qualifications, so you can focus on relaxing. The operator will always confirm exact requirements before you book. The high season runs from June to September, when the Poniente wind often provides steady afternoon breezes ideal for sailing.

For the calmest seas and fewer crowds, consider a trip in May or October; the water is still warm and the famous Levante wind is much less frequent.

At a glance

  • Price band: 85, 5,000 EUR per half-day or full-day charter
  • Peak months: June through September
  • Trip length: 1 hour to full day (8 hours typical)
  • Capacity: 1, 12 guests (most boats)
  • Currency: Euro (EUR)

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

What is the best time of year to charter a boat in Marbella?

The best time is late spring (May) or early autumn (September to October) when temperatures are pleasant and marinas are less crowded.

Can I rent a boat in Marbella without a licence?

Yes, you can rent any boat with a skipper included, which requires no licence on your part; bareboat charters require an ICC.

How far is it from Puerto Banús to the nearest island?

The closest offshore destination is the small nature reserve of Isla de las Palomas, roughly 8 nautical miles south-west of Marbella.

What is included in the crewed yacht charter price?

Most crewed charters include a skipper, fuel, insurance, and sometimes a host; food and drinks are often extra unless specified.
